---
title: "Fledglings"
author: "Mara Bodenmann"
date: today
toc: true
abstract-title: "Research question:"
abstract: "How do environmental stressors and resource availability influence reproductive success?"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true #single HTML file with no supportive directory
#    css: ../../styles.css
    number-sections: true
    code-fold: show
editor: visual
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
execute:
  warning: false #this prevent warning messages being rendered
  message: false
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
1 + 1
```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r}
#| echo: false
2 * 2
```

The `echo: false` option disables the printing of code (only output is displayed).

## Prepare Session

Import libraries

```{r}
#| label: load-libraries

  library(pastecs)
  library(ggpubr); theme_set(theme_bw())
  library(GGally)
  library(performance)
  library(MuMIn)
  library(ggeffects)
  library(tidyverse)

  library(knitr) 
```

Import the data

```{r}
#| label: import-data

clean <- read_csv("data/processed/clean.CSV")

```

Modify the data

```{r}
#| label: modify-data

#deal with NA--> remove the row with this NA
fledglings<- clean%>%
  select(n_fledglings, insect_abundance, env_noise)%>%
  na.omit() #removes rows with na, can specify column: filter(!is.na(column_name))

```

Here the flags and the row with the NA was removed.

## Explore the data

### Numerically

```{r}
#| label: tbl-numerical-exploration-of-data
#| tbl-cap: "Numerical exploration of the data"

numexp<-stat.desc(fledglings,norm = T)

kable(numexp, digits=1)
```

### Graphically

```{r}
#| label: fig-graphical-exploration

expgraph<-ggarrange(
  ggplot(fledglings, aes(x=insect_abundance,y=n_fledglings))+
    geom_point()+
    geom_smooth(method= glm, method.args= list(family= "poisson"),
                  col= "black")+
    labs(x=expression("Insect abundance in (g/m" ^2*")"), y="Number of succesfully raised fledglings per nest"),
  ggplot(fledglings, aes(x=env_noise,y=n_fledglings))+
    geom_point()+
    geom_smooth(method= glm, method.args= list(family= "poisson"),
                  col= "black")+
    labs(x="Environmental ambient noise (db)", y="Number of succesfully raised fledglings per nest")
)
expgraph
```

## Fit the models

Fitting different models to see which one is most suitable for the data

```{r}
#| label: fitting-the-models
mod1<-glm(n_fledglings~ 1, family= "poisson", data=fledglings)
mod2<-glm(n_fledglings~ insect_abundance + env_noise , family= "poisson", data=fledglings)
mod3<-glm(n_fledglings~ insect_abundance * env_noise , family= "poisson", data=fledglings)
```

### Evaluate the models 

Choose which one fits the best.

```{r}
#| label: tbl-evaluate-the-models
#| tbl-cap: "A table showing he evaluation of the models 1-3"

anv.models<-anova(mod1,mod2,mod3)
kable(anv.models)
```

The model I choose is model 2 where there is no interaction between the insect abundance and the environment noise. Because with the interaction, the model was not significantly better than the one without the interaction.
